# Brief 05 — Cohort-based course, mild asymmetry

**Outcome:** Fill 3 cohorts of 40 seats each (120 total) at €1,200/seat over the next 8 months. Course teaches practical AI-assisted engineering for mid-career software engineers.

**Audience:** Senior software engineers (5-15 years experience) at European companies, feeling pressure to adopt AI tooling but wary of hype. Earn €60-100k, willing to invest in skills with clear ROI.

**Sector:** Professional education, cohort-based course.

**Budget:** €20,000 over 8 months. Teacher is the founder; she's a known engineer with 14k LinkedIn followers, 4k newsletter subs, runs a popular open-source project. One part-time ops person.

**Competition:** Major course platforms (Udemy, Coursera) at €20-50/course, and bootcamps at €5k+. A few cohort courses in the niche from US-based teachers at $2k+. Platforms have unlimited marketing budget; cohort courses spend €5-15k per cohort on ads.

**Existing channels:** Newsletter (4k subs, 38% open), LinkedIn (14k followers, average post reach 8k), GitHub (popular OSS, 12k stars). Prior cohort (pilot, 15 students) had 9/15 referrals from her existing network. Testimonials and case studies available.

**Bottleneck:** Scale past her existing network. Needs to reach engineers who don't follow her yet but would trust her once they encounter her work.

**Time window:** 8 months, 3 cohort launches at ~2.5-month intervals.

**Capacity:** Founder 8 hrs/week on marketing (full-time teaching during cohorts), ops person 10 hrs/week.
